metadata.rationale: mission-critical code and exploit-oriented work should use the slower cold CPU lane so it does not compete with the GPU hot-path orchestrator

blocked_reasons: No AD build/version evidence in banners or service discovery to confirm EDB-32586 applicability.
EDB-32586 requires specific AD configuration (e.g., unauthenticated LDAP search enabled); not verified.
No evidence of vulnerable LDAP filter handling (e.g., LDAP_MATCHING_RULE_IN_CHAIN) observed.
PoC research retained only one candidate with partial confidence (0.68) and explicit note of version validation need.

candidate_actions.rationale: EDB-32586 targets username enumeration via LDAP search; applicability depends on AD version and whether unauthenticated binds are allowed. Version detection via LDAP rootDSE or supportedCapabilities may confirm PoC relevance.
candidate_actions.title: Verify AD/LDAP version via unauthenticated LDAP bind + extended control

candidate_actions.rationale: EDB-32586 requires unauthenticated enumeration capability. A bounded test (e.g., (sAMAccountName=*)) with limited page size can confirm feasibility without full PoC execution.
candidate_actions.title: Test LDAP anonymous bind and enumeration with bounded filters

candidate_actions.rationale: IIS 10.0 is present; matching AD schema version (e.g., via /adfs/services/trust/2005/windowstransport) may infer AD build level and PoC compatibility.
candidate_actions.title: Cross-reference IIS version with AD schema version via HTTP headers or /iisstart.htm

capability_gaps: No unauthenticated LDAP enumeration primitive available in current runtime.
No AD schema version detection capability.
No LDAP extended control (e.g., LDAP_SERVER_SORT_OID, LDAP_SERVER_PAGED_RESULTS_OID) support confirmed.
facts: Target pirate.htb hosts Microsoft Active Directory LDAP service on ports 389, 636, 3268, and 3269 (evidence_1fdeeefec3c9, evidence_c0ac9f0d78ce).
LDAP banners confirm 'Microsoft Windows Active Directory LDAP Domain: pirate.htb' but omit version/build details (evidence_1fdeeefec3c9).
HTTP service on port 80 returns IIS 10.0 banner (evidence_c0ac9f0d78ce), consistent with Windows Server 2016/2019/2022 but not definitive.
Searchsploit returned one non-DoS LDAP enumeration PoC (EDB-32586), classified as 'ready_for_review' with version validation pending (interest_83c401e289f2, classified_candidates).
No version-specific evidence (e.g., OS version, AD schema version, patch level) was observed in banners or service discovery.
AXFR DNS enumeration failed, limiting hostname/service discovery (evidence_449a52b5731e).
stop_conditions: Do not execute PoC code or attempt exploitation.
Do not perform unbounded enumeration (e.g., full user dump).
Do not trigger rate-limiting or detection mechanisms.
Cease if LDAP bind fails or returns error code 0x32 (inappropriate authentication).
Cease if search returns >100 entries without pagination control.
ai_proposal.summary: PoC applicability review for Microsoft Active Directory LDAP Username Enumeration (EDB-32586) on pirate.htb indicates partial surface alignment but insufficient version/configuration evidence to confirm applicability. No execution or verification is performed per instructions.

metadata.provider_rationale: mission-critical code and exploit-oriented work should use the slower cold CPU lane so it does not compete with the GPU hot-path orchestrator

task.summary: Classify retained public exploit references against exact service/version evidence, foothold prerequisites, and policy gates for pirate.htb.
task.title: Validate public PoC applicability
